layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto — patience beats panic right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is in a sharp downturn with Bitcoin threatening to break below $62,000 amid geopolitical tensions around the Hormuz Strait. The Fear & Greed index sits at 22 (Extreme Fear), meaning most investors are scared and selling. While extreme fear has historically been a good time to buy, none of the current candidates offer a clean, high-conviction setup with proper risk/reward. The best approach right now is to wait for the market to stabilize before committing capital.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch. Wait for Bitcoin to find a clear floor — likely around $60,000 — before looking for altcoin entries. Patience here protects your capital for a better setup.
expert_reasoning: The market environment is hostile for new altcoin entries: BTC is down 2.7% on the week with geopolitical headlines adding selling pressure, BTC dominance at 56% signals capital rotating out of alts, and Fear & Greed at 22 is improving but not yet capitulated to the degree that marks a reliable bottom. Among candidates: GRASS is down 32% in 7 days but has no chart data or RSI to anchor a stop-loss. BONK has an RSI near oversold (33) but the chart bias is -1.4 (strongly bearish) with a bearish marubozu — a falling knife. LIT and VELVET are overbought/extended after huge 5-day runs. KITE shows a bullish engulfing but is in a downtrend with poor support visibility. JTO is the cleanest setup but at RSI 59 with only 1.05x R:R, it does not meet our minimum threshold for a high-quality swing entry. The disciplined call is to wait.
risk_factors: Bitcoin could break below $60K on Hormuz Strait escalation, dragging all alts significantly lower
BTC dominance at 56% means altcoins are underperforming — capital continues fleeing to Bitcoin or stables
No candidate has volume confirmation or MACD bullish cross — entering without these signals historically leads to poor entries
watch_list: Bitcoin price action around the $60,000 support level — a bounce here would green-light alt entries
Fear & Greed index — if it drops below 15 and then reverses, that's a classic capitulation buy signal
JTO and MON for potential setups if they pull back to key support ($0.60 and $0.0185 respectively) with RSI dropping below 30
market_context: Crypto markets are in a fear-driven selloff with BTC down to $62.4K amid geopolitical tensions and broader risk-asset weakness. High BTC dominance (56%) and Extreme Fear (22/100) suggest alts remain vulnerable but a contrarian opportunity may emerge once selling exhausts.
context_banner: Extreme Fear at 22 with BTC threatening $60K — capital preservation beats speculative entries until a clear bottom forms.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 with improving trend — historically a contrarian buy zone, but we need stabilization first before deploying capital.
Trump's Hormuz Strait comments adding geopolitical risk premium to markets, pushing BTC toward $62K and triggering broad risk-off selling across crypto.
Bitcoin panic-selling may be ending as sellers' profit margins disappear — suggests we're approaching a local bottom but not confirmed yet.
Best candidates show mixed RSI readings: BONK at 33 (near oversold but bearish chart), MON at 37 (approaching oversold), JTO at 59 (already bounced) — no clean oversold bounce setup available.
All candidates below major MAs except JTO and LIT; BONK shows bearish marubozu (-1.4 bias), KITE shows bullish engulfing but in a downtrend — no high-conviction reversal pattern confirmed.
